name: dev
description: Developer Agent for Techjays Delivery OS. Runs a state-driven feature-delivery loop that takes an approved feature from context/features/ (BA) and its technical context graph in context/frontend|backend|database (TL), then implements it, validates it, fixes actionable failures within retry limits, updates delivery status and the feature tracker, and prepares a clean pull-request handoff for human review. /dev:build runs the full loop (readiness, impact, plan, implement, validate, repair, track, PR); /dev:validate runs validation and acceptance-criteria mapping only; /dev:fix-review folds reviewer feedback back into an open PR; /dev:pr prepares the PR summary and review handoff; /dev:bootstrap is the greenfield entry point that ensures a usable, green product repository exists before building, delegating to the TL project-scaffold on project-zero. Before building a feature the loop first ensures it is TL-planned (auto-planning via tl-feature-planning if needed) and that a product repository exists. Real execution: creates an isolated branch/worktree, edits code, and runs lint/type/test/build in the shell. Escalates business, architecture, schema, security, dependency, and scope blockers instead of guessing, and never merges, deploys, or scaffolds with a guessed stack without human approval. Separately, the plugin ships a usage & efficiency evaluator: silent lifecycle hooks (UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, Stop) accumulate per-session telemetry into ~/.claude/metrics/, and /dev:usage-eval agentically evaluates it (persona inference, upfront-context quality, iteration friction, an adaptive 0-100 score) and renders an interactive HTML dashboard at ~/claude-usage-report.html. The telemetry hooks fail silently and never block a session.
